volumeTitle: Antigone. The Women of Trachis. Philoctetes. Oedipus at Colonus
label: Oedipus at Colonus (Section 1.1235 - Section 1435)

sections.label: Section 1.1235
sections.original: καὶ φόνοι · τό τε κατάμεμπτον ἐπιλέλογχε
sections.translation: and murders . Last of all falls to his lot old age , blamed , weak , unsociable , friendless , wherein dwells every misery among miseries .

sections.label: Section 1239
sections.original: ἐν ᾧ τλάμων ὅδʼ , οὐκ ἐγὼ μόνος ,
sections.translation: In such years is this poor man here , not I alone .

sections.label: Section 1240
sections.original: πάντοθεν βόρειος ὥς τις
sections.translation: Like some cape that fronts the north which is lashed on every side by the waves of winter , so he also is fiercely lashed evermore by the dread disasters that break on him like the surf , some from the region of the setting sun ,

sections.label: Section 1245
sections.original: αἱ μὲν ἀπʼ ἀελίου δυσμᾶν ,
sections.translation: some from that of its rising , some in the realm of its noon-time rays , some from the gloom-wrapped hills of the North .

sections.label: Section 1249
sections.original: καὶ μὴν ὅδʼ ἡμῖν , ὡς ἔοικεν , ὁ ξένος
sections.translation: Look , the stranger , it seems , is coming here to us .

sections.label: Section 1250
sections.original: ἀνδρῶν γε μοῦνος , ὦ πάτερ , διʼ ὄμματος
sections.translation: Yes , without attendants , father , with tears streaming from his eyes .

sections.label: Section 1252
sections.original: τίς οὗτος ;
sections.translation: Who is he ?

sections.label: Section 1252a
sections.original: ὅνπερ καὶ πάλαι κατείχομεν
sections.translation: The very man who was in our thoughts from the first . Polyneices has come to us .

sections.label: Section 1254
sections.original: οἴμοι , τί δράσω ; πότερα τἀμαυτοῦ κακὰ
sections.translation: Ah , me , what should I do ? Should I weep first

sections.label: Section 1255
sections.original: πρόσθεν δακρύσω , παῖδες , ἢ τὰ τοῦδʼ ὁρῶν
sections.translation: for my own woes , sisters , or for those of my father here , in his old age ? I have found him in a foreign land , here with you two as an exile , clad in such garments as these . Their unfriendly filth has resided with the old man for long ,

sections.label: Section 1260
sections.original: πλευρὰν μαραίνων , κρατὶ δʼ ὀμματοστερεῖ
sections.translation:
wasting his flesh ; while above the sightless eyes the unkempt hair flutters in the breeze ; and matching with these things , it seems , is the food that he carries , sustenance for his poor stomach .
Wretch that I am ! I learn all this too late .

sections.label: Section 1265
sections.original: καὶ μαρτυρῶ κάκιστος ἀνθρώπων τροφαῖς
sections.translation: And I bear witness that I have proved the worst of men in all that concerns care for you ; from my own lips hear what I am . But seeing that Zeus himself in all his actions has Shame beside him to share his throne , may she come to your aid too , father . For the sins committed can be healed ,

sections.label: Section 1270
sections.original: ἄκη μέν ἐστι , προσφορὰ δʼ οὐκ ἔστʼ ἔτι .
sections.translation:
but can never be made worse .
Why are you silent ? Speak , father . Do not turn away from me . Do you not have any answer at all for me ? Will you dismiss me without a word , dishonored , and not tell me why you are angry ?

sections.label: Section 1275
sections.original: ὦ σπέρματʼ ἀνδρὸς τοῦδʼ , ἐμαὶ δʼ ὁμαίμονες ,
sections.translation: Seed of this man , my sisters , you at least must try to move our father ’ s implacable , inexorable silence , so that he may not send me away like this , dishonored and with no word in return , when I am the suppliant of the god .

sections.label: Section 1280
sections.original: λέγʼ , ὦ ταλαίπωρʼ , αὐτὸς ὧν χρείᾳ πάρει ·
sections.translation: Tell him yourself , unhappy man , what you have come to seek . When words flow , you know , they may give joy , or incite anger or pity , and so they may give a voice to the mute .

sections.label: Section 1284
sections.original: ἀλλʼ ἐξερῶ · καλῶς γὰρ ἐξηγεῖ σύ μοι ·
sections.translation: Then I will speak boldly , for you give me excellent guidance ,

sections.label: Section 1285
sections.original: πρῶτον μὲν αὐτὸν τὸν θεὸν ποιούμενος
sections.translation: first claiming the help of the god himself , from whose altar the king of this land raised me to come to you , with a guarantee to speak and hear , and go my way unharmed . And I wish these pledges , strangers , to be kept with me by you , and by my sisters here , and by my father .

sections.label: Section 1290
sections.original: καὶ ταῖνδʼ ἀδελφαῖν καὶ πατρὸς κυρεῖν ἐμοί .
sections.translation:
But now I want to tell you , father , why I came .
I have been driven as an exile from my fatherland , because , as eldest-born , I thought it right to sit on your sovereign throne .

sections.label: Section 1295
sections.original: ἀνθʼ ὧν μʼ Ἐτεοκλῆς , ὢν φύσει νεώτερος ,
sections.translation: Therefore Eteocles , though the younger , thrust me from the land , when he had neither defeated me by an argument of law , nor made a trial of might and deed . He brought over the city by persuasion . The cause of this , I claim , is most of all the curse on your house ;

sections.label: Section 1300
sections.original: ἔπειτα κἀπὸ μάντεων ταύτῃ κλύω .
sections.translation: I also hear this from soothsayers . For when I came to Dorian Argos , I made Adrastus my father-in-law . And I bound to me by oath all men of the Apian land who are foremost in their renown for war ,

sections.label: Section 1305
sections.original: ὅπως τὸν ἑπτάλογχον ἐς Θήβας στόλον
sections.translation:
so that with their aid I might collect the seven armies of spearmen against Thebes , and die in a just cause , or drive the doers of this wrong from the land .
All right then , why have I come to you now ? Bearing prayers of supplication , father , in person to you ,

sections.label: Section 1310
sections.original: αὐτός τʼ ἐμαυτοῦ ξυμμάχων τε τῶν ἐμῶν ,
sections.translation: my own prayers and those of my allies , who now with seven armies behind their seven spears have set their blockade around the plain of Thebes . One such is swift-speared Amphiaraus , a matchless warrior , and a matchless diviner ;

sections.label: Section 1315
sections.original: ὁ δεύτερος δʼ Αἰτωλὸς Οἰνέως τόκος
sections.translation: then comes the son of Oeneus , Aetolian Tydeus ; Eteoclus is third , of Argive birth ; the fourth , Hippomedon , is sent by Talaos , his father ; while Capaneus , the fifth , boasts that he will burn Thebes to the ground with fire ; and sixth , Arcadian Parthenopaeus rushes to the war .

sections.label: Section 1320
sections.original: ἕκτος δὲ Παρθενοπαῖος Ἀρκὰς ὄρνυται ,
sections.translation: He is named for that virgin of long ago from whose marriage in later time he was born , the trusty son of Atalanta . Last come I , your son — or if not yours , then the offspring of an evil fate , but yours at least in name —

sections.label: Section 1325
sections.original: ἄγω τὸν Ἄργους ἄφοβον ἐς Θήβας στρατόν .
sections.translation:
leading the fearless army of Argos to Thebes .
It is we who implore you , father , every one of us , by your daughters here and by your soul , begging you to forgo your fierce anger against me , as I go forth to punish my brother ,

sections.label: Section 1330
sections.original: ὅς μʼ ἐξέωσε κἀπεσύλησεν πάτρας .
sections.translation:
who has expelled me and robbed me of my fatherland . For if anything trustworthy comes from oracles , they said that whoever you join with in alliance will have victorious strength .
Then , by the streams of water and gods of our race , I ask you to listen and to yield .

sections.label: Section 1335
sections.original: πτωχοὶ μὲν ἡμεῖς καὶ ξένοι , ξένος δὲ σύ .
sections.translation: I am a beggar and a stranger , as you are yourself ; by paying court to others both you and I have a home , obtaining by lot the same fortune . But he is tyrant at home — wretched me !— and in his pride laughs at you and me alike .

sections.label: Section 1340
sections.original: ὅν , εἰ σὺ τἠμῇ ξυμπαραστήσει φρενί ,
sections.translation: But if you join as ally to my purpose , with little trouble or time I will scatter his strength to the winds , so that I will bring you home and set you in your own house , and set me in mine , when I have cast him out by force . If you are with me , then I can make this boast ; but without you

sections.label: Section 1345
sections.original: κομπεῖν , ἄνευ σοῦ δʼ οὐδὲ σωθῆναι σθένω .
sections.translation: I cannot even return alive .

sections.label: Section 1346
sections.original: τὸν ἄνδρα τοῦ πέμψαντος οὕνεκʼ , Οἰδίπους
sections.translation: For the sake of him who has sent this man , Oedipus , speak what seems good to you , before you send him away .

sections.label: Section 1348
sections.original: ἀλλʼ εἰ μέν , ἄνδρες , τῆσδε δημοῦχοι χθονός
sections.translation: Guardians of this land , if it were not Theseus who had sent him here to me , thinking it just that he should hear my response ,

sections.label: Section 1350
sections.original: Θησεύς , δικαιῶν ὥστʼ ἐμοῦ κλύειν λόγους ,
sections.translation: then never would he have heard my voice of prophecy . But now he will be graced with it , before he goes , and hear from me such words as never will gladden his life .

sections.label: Section 1355
sections.original: ἃ νῦν ὁ σὸς ξύναιμος ἐν Θήβαις ἔχει ,
sections.translation: Worst of men , when you had the scepter and the throne , which now your brother has in Thebes , you drove me , your own father , into exile ; and by making me an exile you caused me to wear this clothing at whose sight you weep , now that you have come to the same state of misery as I .

sections.label: Section 1360
sections.original: οὐ κλαυστὰ δʼ ἐστίν , ἀλλʼ ἐμοὶ μὲν οἰστέα
sections.translation: The time for tears is past . I must bear this burden as long as I live , and keep you before my mind as a murderer . For it is you that have made me subject to this anguish ; it is you that have thrust me out , and because of you I wander , begging my daily bread from strangers .

sections.label: Section 1365
sections.original: εἰ δʼ ἐξέφυσα τάσδε μὴ ʼμαυτῷ τροφοὺς
sections.translation: And had these daughters not been born to me to be my comfort , in truth I would be dead , for lack of help from you . But now these girls preserve me ; they are my nurses ; they are men , not women , in sharing my toil . But you are from another and are no sons of mine .

sections.label: Section 1370
sections.original: τοιγάρ σʼ ὁ δαίμων εἰσορᾷ μὲν οὔ τί πω
sections.translation: Therefore the divinity looks upon you — not yet as he soon will look , if indeed those armies of yours are moving against Thebes . There is no way in which you can ever overthrow that city . Before that you will fall , polluted by bloodshed , and so too your brother .

sections.label: Section 1375
sections.original: τοιάσδʼ ἀρὰς σφῷν πρόσθε τʼ ἐξανῆκʼ ἐγὼ
sections.translation: Such curses as my heart before now sent up against you both , I now invoke to fight for me , in order that you may think it fit to revere your parents and not to dishonor your father utterly , because he who begot such sons is blind . For my daughters here did not act in this way .

sections.label: Section 1380
sections.original: τοιγὰρ τὸ σὸν θάκημα καὶ τοὺς σοὺς θρόνους
sections.translation:
This supplication of yours , and this throne of yours , will lie in the power of my curses , if indeed Justice , revealed long ago , sits beside Zeus , to share his throne through sanction of primordial laws .
But off to damnation with you , abhorred by me and disowned !

sections.label: Section 1385
sections.original: ἅς σοι καλοῦμαι , μήτε γῆς ἐμφυλίου
sections.translation: Take these curses which I call down on you , most evil of evil men : may you never defeat your native land , and may you never return to the valley of Argos ; I pray that you die by a related hand , and slay him by whom you have been driven out . This is my prayer .

sections.label: Section 1390
sections.original: στυγνὸν πατρῷον ἔρεβος , ὥς σʼ ἀποικίσῃ ,
sections.translation: And I call on the hateful darkness of Tartarus that your father shares , to take you into another home ; and I call on the divinities of this place , and I call on the god of war , who has set dreadful hatred in you both . Go with these words in your ear ;

sections.label: Section 1395
sections.original: πιστοῖσι συμμάχοισιν , οὕνεκʼ Οἰδίπους
sections.translation: go and announce to all the Cadmeans , and to your own faithful allies , that Oedipus has distributed such portions to his sons .

sections.label: Section 1397
sections.original: Πολύνεικες , οὔτε ταῖς παρελθούσαις ὁδοῖς
sections.translation: Polyneices , in your past travels I take no joy . Now go back with speed .

sections.label: Section 1399
sections.original: οἴμοι κελεύθου τῆς τʼ ἐμῆς δυσπραξίας ,
sections.translation: Alas , for my journey and my failed attempt ! Alas , for my companions !

sections.label: Section 1400
sections.original: οἴμοι δʼ ἑταίρων · οἷον ἆρʼ ὁδοῦ τέλος
sections.translation: Such is the end of the road on which we set out from Argos — wretched me !— such an end , that I cannot even mention it to any of my companions or turn them back , but must go in silence to meet this fate .

sections.label: Section 1405
sections.original: ὦ τοῦδʼ ὅμαιμοι παῖδες , ἀλλʼ ὑμεῖς , ἐπεὶ
sections.translation: But you , daughters of this man and my sisters , since you hear these hard curses of a father , do not — if this father ’ s curses be fulfilled and you find some way of return to Thebes — do not , I beg you by the gods , leave me dishonored ,

sections.label: Section 1410
sections.original: ἀλλʼ ἐν τάφοισι θέσθε κἀν κτερίσμασιν .
sections.translation: but give me burial and due funeral rites . So the praise which you now win from this man here for your labors will be increased by another praise no less , through your care for me .

sections.label: Section 1414
sections.original: Πολύνεικες , ἱκετεύω σε πεισθῆναί τί μοι .
sections.translation: Polyneices , I beseech you , hear me in one thing !

sections.label: Section 1415
sections.original: ὦ φιλτάτη , τὸ ποῖον , Ἀντιγόνη ; λέγε .
sections.translation: What is it , dearest Antigone ? Speak !

sections.label: Section 1416
sections.original: στρέψαι στράτευμʼ ἐς Ἄργος ὡς τάχιστά γε ,
sections.translation: Turn your force back to Argos as quickly as may be , and do not destroy both yourself and your city .

sections.label: Section 1418
sections.original: ἀλλʼ οὐχ οἷόν τε · πῶς γὰρ αὖθις ἂν πάλιν
sections.translation: No , it is not possible . For how could I lead the same force again , when once I had shrunk back ?

sections.label: Section 1420
sections.original: τί δʼ αὖθις , ὦ παῖ , δεῖ σε θυμοῦσθαι ; τί σοι
sections.translation: But why , my brother , must your anger rise again ? What profit will come to you from destroying your native land ?

sections.label: Section 1422
sections.original: αἰσχρὸν τὸ φεύγειν καὶ τὸ πρεσβεύοντʼ ἐμὲ
sections.translation: It is shameful to be in exile , and to be mocked in this way by my brother , when I am eldest-born .

sections.label: Section 1424
sections.original: ὁρᾷς τὰ τοῦδʼ οὖν ὡς ἐς ὀρθὸν ἐκφέρει
sections.translation: Do you see to what sure fulfillment the prophecies of this man are leading ,

sections.label: Section 1425
sections.original: μαντεύμαθʼ , ὃς σφῷν θάνατον ἐξ ἀμφοῖν θροεῖ ;
sections.translation: who declares mutual death for you two ?

sections.label: Section 1426
sections.original: χρῄζει γάρ · ἡμῖν δʼ οὐχὶ συγχωρητέα .
sections.translation: Yes , for he wishes it . But I must not yield .

sections.label: Section 1427
sections.original: οἴμοι τάλαινα · τίς δὲ τολμήσει κλύων
sections.translation: Ah , wretched me ! But who will dare follow you , when he hears what prophecies this man has uttered ?

sections.label: Section 1429
sections.original: οὐκ ἀγγελοῦμεν φλαῦρʼ · ἐπεὶ στρατηλάτου
sections.translation: I will not report ill-tidings ; a good leader should tell the better news , and not the worse .

sections.label: Section 1431
sections.original: οὕτως ἄρʼ , ὦ παῖ , ταῦτά σοι δεδογμένα ;
sections.translation: Is this then your fixed decision , my brother ?

sections.label: Section 1432
sections.original: καὶ μή μʼ ἐπίσχῃς γʼ · ἀλλʼ ἐμοὶ μὲν ἥδʼ ὁδὸς
sections.translation: Yes , and do not detain me . This path now will be my destiny , ill-fated and evil , because of my father here and his Furies . But as for you two ,

sections.label: Section 1435
sections.original: σφῷν δʼ εὖ διδοίη Ζεύς , τάδʼ εἰ θανόντι μοι
sections.translation: may Zeus grant you good things , if you bring these things to completion for me when I am dead , since in life you will see me no more .
